projectDescription: WiVRn is a fully open-source PCVR streaming application that wirelessly streams VR games from a Linux PC to standalone Android VR headsets via OpenXR.
keyFeatures: Wireless VR streaming from Linux PC to standalone Android headsets
OpenXR runtime with support for Quest, Pico, HTC Vive, and Samsung XR headsets
Hardware video encoding via NVENC (Nvidia), VAAPI (AMD/Intel), Vulkan Video, and software x264
Qt6 dashboard GUI with setup wizard and headset management
OpenVR/Steam game compatibility via xrizer and OpenComposite
mDNS/Avahi-based automatic server discovery on local network
Audio streaming with virtual PipeWire/PulseAudio devices
HID input forwarding (keyboard/mouse from headset to PC)
publicApiSurface: wivrn-dashboard — graphical dashboard and server manager
config.json — JSON configuration file (encoder, codec, application, tcp-only, port, hostname, etc.)
WIVRN_DUMP_VIDEO env var — dump video frames for analysis
WIVRN_DUMP_TIMINGS env var — dump timing CSV
XRT_LOG / XRT_COMPOSITOR_LOG env vars — log level control
wivrn:// URI scheme — deep-link connection from headset
ADB reverse tunnel: adb reverse tcp:9757 tcp:9757
